statement: Do not merge all conditions into one entry merely because the author introduced earlier conditions elsewhere.
Do not assume a small note about earlier conditions is always enough; continued-topic support may need to be stronger.
Do not apply the short-and-harmless rule blindly.
Do not keep two unrelated شروط together merely because the extra text is short.
Do not ignore same-branch vs distant-branch proximity when deciding whether retained overlap is harmless.
Do not tolerate ambiguous internal vocabulary for core structural objects.
Do not let the library behave like a thin wrapper over books if continued topics and condition structure can be surfaced more intelligently.
Do not suppress condition-level sub-excerpting merely because the resumed block is short overall.
Do not treat continuation as a reason to force one mega-entry when stronger continuation support can preserve both structure and reassurance.
Do not use non-Arabic internal naming in the actual library taxonomy and titles.
